response:
we must store the value of the factorial to begin our analysis of the problem.
***
the number one hundred factorial contains many factors that we must store in our memory.
***
to find the denominator we need to store the count of prime factors in the numerator.
***
we store the fact that the power of ten is determined by the number of fives.
***
calculating the exponent of five allows us to store the exact reduction needed for the fraction.
***
the denominator will be a power of two because we store the remaining factors after division.
***
we store the result that the exponent of five in one hundred factorial is twenty four.
***
since we store twenty four fives and need fifty zeros, we must divide by two to the power of twenty six.
***
the final answer requires us to store the value of two raised to the power of twenty six.

we have successfully stored the solution which is the denominator of the irreducible fraction.
